description: PF-7, PF-1, PF-2, PF-6: Blocked subsystem implementations for zuicchini port

rules.immutability: Agent MAY set passes to true. Agent MAY NOT delete features, modify steps, or mark features as not applicable.
rules.gate_protocol: A feature's passes field may only be set to true after: (1) implementation compiles with zero clippy warnings, (2) the feature's verification steps all succeed, (3) all previously-passing features still pass.
rules.commit_protocol: Commit after each feature passes its gate. Message format: feat: <feature_id> <short description>
rules.execution_order: Features must be implemented in the order listed. Dependencies are explicit.

features.description: EgoMode: cursor override to crosshair + scroll clamping to viewport center

Add if-check in View cursor method: when EGO_MODE active and cursor is Normal, return Crosshair
Add if-check in raw_scroll_and_zoom: when EGO_MODE active, collapse clamp boundaries to viewport center
Toggle flag invalidates cursor
Write pipeline test: :egomode! toggles ViewFlags::EGO_MODE
Write integration test: with EGO_MODE active, scroll attempt does not change viewport center position

In View::paint(), after all panel painting, call stress_test.paint_info() to render 'Stress Test XX.X Hz' overlay
In app loop, when STRESS_TEST active, bypass dirty-rect check (force full repaint every frame)
Frame rate computed as count * 1000.0 / elapsed_ms over entries within last 1 second, updated every 100ms

Write golden test: render a view with STRESS_TEST active after a few ticks, verify the overlay text 'Stress Test' appears in the output pixels (use compare_images with the rendered buffer)

features.description: Velocity handoff during Activate() — walk active chain, find outgoing KVA, copy velocity
steps: Read C++ emKineticViewAnimator::Activate() (emViewAnimator.cpp:188-226) — walks GetActiveAnimator chain with dynamic_cast to find outgoing KVA
In Rust KineticViewAnimator::activate() (or equivalent), before calling base activate: walk View.active_animator chain to find any active KineticViewAnimator
If found: call extract_kinetic_state() on the outgoing animator, inject into self
If not found: zero all velocity fields
Match C++ zoom fix point logic: if self.zoom_fix_centered, call center_zoom_fix_point() after injection; else call set_zoom_fix_point(fix_x, fix_y)

features.description: CalculateDistance — panel tree DFS to find nearest focusable panel with 3D distance
steps: Read C++ emMagneticViewAnimator::CalculateDistance (emViewAnimator.cpp:809-907)
Early return if VF_POPUP_ZOOM (magnetism not functioning with popup zoom, per C++ comment)
Get supreme viewed panel from View
DFS walk from supreme viewed panel: for each viewed + focusable panel, compute essence rect in view coords via panel_to_view_x/y/delta_x/delta_y
For each candidate: tx = panel_center_x - view_center_x, ty = panel_center_y - view_center_y, tz = log(view_dim / panel_dim) / zflpp where zflpp = View.get_zoom_factor_logarithm_per_pixel()
Track minimum td = tx*tx + ty*ty + tz*tz across all candidates
Return (dx, dy, dz, sqrt(td))
Write unit test: panel tree with 3 focusable panels at different positions, verify nearest is found correctly
Write unit test: verify distance metric uses log-zoom for z-axis
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings: zero warnings
cargo nextest run --workspace: zero failures

features.description: CycleAnimation magnetism enter/exit logic based on distance vs radius threshold
steps: Read C++ emMagneticViewAnimator::CycleAnimation (emViewAnimator.cpp:716-806) — magnetism activation section (lines 716-755)
Read CoreConfig magnetism_radius and magnetism_speed fields
Compute maxDist = (view_w + view_h) * 0.09 * radius_factor. If radius_factor <= min_value * 1.0001, maxDist = 0.0 (magnetism disabled)
If abs_dist <= maxDist and abs_dist > 1e-3: if not yet magnetism_active and abs_velocity < 10.0, center zoom fix point, set magnetism_active = true. Set busy = true
If abs_dist > maxDist or abs_dist <= 1e-3: if magnetism_active, zero all velocities, set magnetism_active = false. If abs_velocity >= 0.01, set busy = true (friction deceleration)
Write unit test: panel within magnetism radius activates magnetism
Write unit test: panel outside radius does not activate magnetism
Write unit test: high velocity (>10.0) prevents magnetism activation even within radius
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings: zero warnings
cargo nextest run --workspace: zero failures
Read C++ emMagneticViewAnimator::CycleAnimation physics section (emViewAnimator.cpp:757-797)
When magnetism_active: if speed_factor >= max * 0.9999 or abs_dist < 1.0, use instant velocity v = abs_dist / dt

Otherwise: sub-stepped Euler integration loop. fdt = min(dt - t, 0.01). Slope k = (abs_dist - d) / max_dist * 4.0, clamped to [-1,1] via k = 1.0/k if |k|>1.0. Acceleration a = k * max_dist * 25.0 * speed_factor^2. Damping: a -= |v| * 15.0 * speed_factor. v += a * fdt, d += v * fdt. Break if d >= abs_dist.
Final velocity: v = d / dt. Set velocity components proportional to distance direction: v * dx/abs_dist, v * dy/abs_dist, v * dz/abs_dist
Disable friction during base KineticViewAnimator::CycleAnimation when magnetism is active
Write unit test: with magnetism active and panel at distance 100, verify velocity converges toward target over multiple frames
Write unit test: with max speed factor, verify instant snap (v = abs_dist / dt)
Write unit test: verify sub-stepping produces numerically stable results (test with large dt = 0.1)

Write golden trajectory test in tests/golden/animator.rs: create a panel tree with a focusable target panel, activate magnetic animator, run 60 frames, record (x, y, zoom) trajectory, compare against C++ golden trajectory data (add generator function to tests/golden/gen/gen_golden.cpp)
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings: zero warnings
cargo nextest run --workspace: zero failures

features.description: Touch struct + tracking infrastructure (ResetTouches, NextTouches, RemoveTouch, move getters)
steps: Read C++ emDefaultTouchVIF Touch struct (emViewInputFilter.h:286-298) and helper methods (emViewInputFilter.cpp:1214-1303)
Implement Touch struct: id (u64), ms_total (i32), ms_since_prev (i32), down (bool), x/y (f64), prev_down (bool), prev_x/prev_y (f64), down_x/down_y (f64)
Implement touches: [Touch; MAX_TOUCH_COUNT=16], touch_count: usize, touches_time: u64
Implement reset_touches(): zero touch_count, clear touches_time
Implement next_touches(): for each active touch, copy current to prev, update ms_since_prev from delta time
Implement remove_touch(index): shift remaining touches down
Implement is_any_touch_down(): any touch with down == true
Implement get_touch_move_x/y(index), get_total_touch_move_x/y(index) matching C++ (current - prev, current - down)
Write unit test: add touches, verify move calculations
Write unit test: remove_touch shifts correctly
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings: zero warnings
cargo nextest run --workspace: zero failures
Read C++ DoGesture states: STATE_FIRST_DOWN, STATE_SCROLL, STATE_ZOOM_IN, STATE_ZOOM_OUT (emViewInputFilter.cpp:971-1018)

Define GestureState enum with all 17 states matching C++ (Ready, FirstDown, Scroll, ZoomIn, ZoomOut, FirstDownUp, DoubleDown, DoubleDownUp, TripleDown, TripleDownUp, SecondDown, EmuMouse1-4, ThirdDown, FourthDown, Finish)
Implement STATE_FIRST_DOWN: if touch_count > 1 → SecondDown. If !down → FirstDownUp. If total_move > 20.0 → Scroll (call View.scroll). If ms_total > 250 → ZoomIn.
Implement STATE_SCROLL: if !down → Finish. Otherwise call View.scroll(-move_x, -move_y) each frame.
Implement STATE_ZOOM_IN: if !down → Finish. Call View.scroll + View.zoom(x, y, exp(0.002 * ms_since_prev)).
Implement STATE_ZOOM_OUT: same but exp(-0.002 * ms_since_prev).
Write pipeline test: single touch drag > 20px enters scroll state, view position changes
Write pipeline test: single touch hold > 250ms enters zoom-in state
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings: zero warnings
cargo nextest run --workspace: zero failures

features.description: Tap chain: single-tap timeout, double-tap visit-fullsized, triple-tap visit-utilizing
steps: Read C++ DoGesture states: STATE_FIRST_DOWN_UP, STATE_DOUBLE_DOWN, STATE_DOUBLE_DOWN_UP, STATE_TRIPLE_DOWN, STATE_TRIPLE_DOWN_UP (emViewInputFilter.cpp:1019-1077)
STATE_FIRST_DOWN_UP: if new touch arrives (touch_count > 1), remove old touch, transition to DoubleDown. If ms_total > 250, transition to Finish (single-tap timeout).
STATE_DOUBLE_DOWN: if !down → DoubleDownUp. If ms_total > 250 → ZoomOut.
STATE_DOUBLE_DOWN_UP: if new touch → TripleDown. If ms_total > 250 → find focusable panel at touch position, call View.visit_fullsized(panel, true, false), transition to Finish.
STATE_TRIPLE_DOWN: if !down → TripleDownUp. If ms_total > 250 → ZoomIn.
STATE_TRIPLE_DOWN_UP: if new touch → DoubleDown (cycle restarts). If ms_total > 250 → visit_fullsized(panel, true, true), Finish.
Write pipeline test: double-tap (down, up within 250ms, down, up within 250ms, wait 250ms) triggers visit_fullsized
Write pipeline test: single-tap timeout (down, up, wait 250ms) produces no action
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings: zero warnings
cargo nextest run --workspace: zero failures

features.description: Two-finger directional mouse emulation (4 EMU_MOUSE states)
steps: Read C++ DoGesture states: STATE_SECOND_DOWN, STATE_EMU_MOUSE_1-4 (emViewInputFilter.cpp:1078-1177)

STATE_SECOND_DOWN: if touch_count > 2 → ThirdDown. If ms_total > 250 or !any_touch_down: compute dx/dy between two touch points. If |dx| >= |dy|: dx > 0 → left-click emulation (EMU_MOUSE_1), dx < 0 → right-click (EMU_MOUSE_2). If |dy| > |dx|: dy > 0 → shift+left-click (EMU_MOUSE_3), dy < 0 → ctrl+left-click (EMU_MOUSE_4).

For each EMU_MOUSE state: set InputState mouse position to Touches[0] position. Maintain modifier+button state while touch held. On touch release: clear modifiers/buttons, forward release event, transition to Finish.
Use ForwardInput to inject synthetic mouse events into the VIF chain.
Write pipeline test: two fingers placed horizontally right → generates left-click at first finger position
Write pipeline test: two fingers placed horizontally left → generates right-click
Write pipeline test: two fingers placed vertically down → generates shift+left-click
Write pipeline test: two fingers placed vertically up → generates ctrl+left-click
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings: zero warnings
cargo nextest run --workspace: zero failures

features.description: Three-finger menu key + four-finger soft keyboard toggle + finish/ready cycle
steps: Read C++ DoGesture states: STATE_THIRD_DOWN, STATE_FOURTH_DOWN, STATE_FINISH (emViewInputFilter.cpp:1178-1211)
STATE_THIRD_DOWN: if touch_count > 3 → FourthDown. If !any_touch_down → inject Menu key press+release via ForwardInput, transition to Finish.
STATE_FOURTH_DOWN: if touch_count > 4 → Finish. If !any_touch_down → call View.show_soft_keyboard(!View.is_soft_keyboard_shown()), transition to Finish.
STATE_FINISH: if !any_touch_down → reset_touches(), transition to Ready.
Wire DoGesture into Input() and Cycle() methods: Input() updates touch state from events, then loops DoGesture until state stabilizes. Cycle() also loops DoGesture for time-based transitions.
Write pipeline test: three-finger touch-and-release generates Menu key event
Write pipeline test: four-finger touch-and-release toggles soft keyboard
Write pipeline test: after gesture completes and all fingers lift, state returns to Ready
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings: zero warnings
cargo nextest run --workspace: zero failures
